methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $60M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Renaissance Fusion, a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Francesco Volpe and Martin Kupp, has raised $60.5M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 11 peers: 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms including Fuse Energy, X-Energy, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

Stage-progression methodology is applicable and yields a $121M valuation. Using total capital raised ($60.5M) as a proxy for current value and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage progression, this method implies $121M. Peer-median comparison is not applicable: while 4 same-sector peers exist, the narrow peer set filtered by stage, timing, and valuation tier contains fewer than three datapoints, making a median benchmark unreliable and creating undue risk of survivorship or tier bias.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $103M to $139M (median $121M), derived by applying conservative and optimistic case adjustments of ±15% around the stage-progression point estimate. Key uncertainties include the early-stage nature of the sector, which limits comparable transaction data, and the lack of recent private-market financings at similar stages in nuclear energy, creating material visibility gaps around growth-rate expectations and capital intensity assumptions.
